feed.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes

entry.title: Re: March 23rd 2021 Taproot Activation Meeting Notes
entry.summary:
The discussion highlights the flexibility in adjusting project timelines, specifically mentioning the possibility of moving dates back by two weeks. This suggests that there was some ambiguity during a meeting regarding whether the release date was initially set for May 1st or an unspecified date referred to as "startheight." This ambiguity is addressed through the concept of parameter flexibility, indicating that adjustments can be made to align with the team's preferences or requirements.
The selection of the Midpoint Time Preference (MTP) is noted as being based on an estimation rather than arbitrary decision-making, suggesting a methodical approach to determining this aspect of the project timeline. Furthermore, the email downplays the importance of adhering to a strict deadline, such as midnight UTC, by emphasizing that time is perceived differently across global locations ("it's 5 o'clock somewhere"). This implies a pragmatic view towards deadlines, proposing that slight deviations from the planned timing are acceptable and won't significantly impact the project due to the strategic choice of a mid-period MTP. The underlying message is that choosing a mid-period MTP minimizes the effect of time drift on project outcomes, providing a buffer that accommodates minor scheduling adjustments without compromising the project's integrity or timeline.
